notes: BACKFLOW re-spec against the LIVE recording (validation drift; recording committed as-is). ROOT CAUSE (issue #906): the daemon does NOT promote the initial presession (proc-<PID>) to a real session_<id> before /clear rotates it away — so the FIRST session's turn arc is never captured as a real session. The recording bears this out: proc-<PID> is born ready, binds its PID, and its transcript is REMOVED (/clear rotation) BEFORE any real session_<ts> dir ever reconciles; only the POST-/clear session appears as a real session_<id> and it carries the single observable working→ready turn. So the initial session is modeled as a PRESESSION-ONLY arc (presession_birth ready → pid_bind → old presession transcript_removed), and the post-/clear session as the real new_session birth (presession→session_birth) carrying the turn. The original spec over-asserted TWO real sessions each with a full turn arc (v1_session_handoff + v2_session_birth); that never happens on vibe because of #906. BACKFLOW observations: offline replay cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so both are UNVERIFIABLE offline — observations assert agent only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false), matching kiro-cli.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch — the INITIAL session, which #906 never promotes to a real session_<id> before /clear rotates it away
vibe PID bound to the presession within 1s (DiscoverPID by cwd + cmdline)

Spec: 'Old session disappears' — /clear rotates the initial presession away: its transcript is removed BEFORE any real session_<ts> dir reconciles (issue #906: the initial session is never promoted, so its turn arc is never captured)

Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — after /clear a fresh session_<ts2> dir (never seen before) reconciles and is born ready; this is the FIRST real vibe session the daemon captures
The post-/clear prompt drives the NEW real session ready → working — the single observable turn (must NOT match a stale transition on the presession row)
The post-/clear turn completes back to ready on the new session; final state ready, never entered waiting
